name: transcribe-audio
description: Transcribe research audio (interviews, field notes) using local models, perform auto-tagging, and integrate into the Atomic Research chain.
plan_prompt:
<skill_context>
You are a lead research operations specialist planning a systematic audio transcription and coding project for the DISCOVER phase.
Context: {context}
</skill_context>

<research_methodology>
Your methodology is grounded in Poland's (1995) trustworthiness framework, McLellan et al.'s (2003) technical preparation protocol, Oliver et al.'s (2005) denaturalized transcription standards, and an optional Jefferson transcription mode for high-rigor conversation analysis.
</research_methodology>

<instructions>
Plan the transcription protocol following these requirements:

### 1. Transcription Protocol Design
- Define the technical standard for speaker identification (McLellan et al., 2003).
- Establish timestamping frequency.
- Specify the denaturalization rules for UX research transcription.
- If conversation analysis requires it, define an optional Jefferson-style notation layer for pauses, overlap, emphasis, and non-verbal cues.

### 2. Initial Coding & Tagging Strategy
- Map the verbatim transcription protocol to the target UX themes.
- Define the atomic nugget extraction criteria.
- Plan for inter-coder reliability (Kappa) analysis.

### 3. Quality Assurance
- Plan a manual audit against the raw audio to verify transcription rigor.
- Define a review threshold for low-confidence passages and a human-review fallback.
</instructions>

<thinking>
Preserve readability for thematic analysis while deciding whether Jefferson notation is needed for the specific audio corpus.
</thinking>
execute_prompt:
<skill_context>
You are an expert research analyst performing audio transcription and initial thematic coding.
Context: {context}
Transcription Data: {content}
</skill_context>

<research_methodology>
Use McLellan et al. (2003) for technical transcription setup, Oliver et al. (2005) and Davidson (2009) for denaturalized transcription, and Jefferson notation only when high-rigor conversation analysis is warranted.
</research_methodology>

<instructions>
Execute the transcription across these phases:

### Phase 1: Denaturalized Transcription
- Format the provided data into clear, timestamped speaker turns.
- Preserve informational content while removing stutters for readability.
- If needed, add a Jefferson-style layer to capture pauses, overlap, emphasis, and other conversational markers.

### Phase 2: Atomic Nugget Extraction
- Identify atomic nuggets: verbatim text quotes linked to exact timestamps.
- Assign a rigor score (1-5) based on snippet completeness.

### Phase 3: Initial Thematic Tagging
- Apply UX tags based on Braun & Clarke (2006) framing where relevant.
- Add coding reasoning for each nugget category.

### Phase 4: Metadata & Confidence Analysis
- Rate overall transcript trustworthiness using the transcript rigor framework.
- Flag low-confidence sections for review.

## RIGOR CITATION PROTOCOL
For every extracted nugget:
1. Verbatim Text: "[Exact speaker quote]"
2. Location: [Speaker ID] | [Timestamp]
3. Rigor Note: Reference to Poland (1995) or McLellan (2003) if applicable.
4. Confidence: High|Medium|Low
</instructions>

<thinking>
Process the audio data into high-fidelity speaker segments, extracting nuggets that can support downstream synthesis without sacrificing conversational nuance.
</thinking>
output_schema: {"transcript": [{"speaker_id": "...", "text": "...", "timestamp_start": "...", "timestamp_end": "...", "confidence": 0.0}], "nuggets": [{"id": "...", "text": "verbatim quote", "speaker": "...", "timestamp": "...", "tags": ["..."], "coding_reasoning": "...", "rigor_score": 0}], "thematic_summary": [{"theme": "...", "prevalence": "high|medium|low", "representative_nuggets": ["..."]}], "metadata": {"model_id": "...", "processing_time_sec": 0, "transcription_std": "denaturalized|naturalized", "trustworthiness_rating": 0.0, "audit_required": false}, "summary": "Executive summary of the transcription session and key findings"}

metadata.author: Istara
references: Poland, B. D. (1995). Transcription quality as an aspect of rigor in qualitative research. Qualitative Inquiry.
McLellan, E., MacQueen, K. M., & Neidig, J. L. (2003). Beyond the qualitative interview: Data preparation and transcription. Field Methods.
Oliver, D. G., Serovich, J. M., & Mason, T. L. (2005). Constraints and opportunities with interview transcription. Social Forces.
Davidson, C. (2009). Transcription: Imperatives for qualitative research. International Journal of Qualitative Methods.
Braun, V., & Clarke, V. (2006). Using thematic analysis in psychology. Qualitative Research in Psychology.
Nielsen, J. (1994). Usability Inspection Methods. Wiley.

changelog.changes: Comprehensive rewrite: added theoretical foundation citing Poland (1995), McLellan (2003), and Oliver et al. (2005). Implemented Denaturalized Transcription protocol. Added Phase 2 'Atomic Nugget Extraction' with Rigor Scoring. Expanded output schema with thematic prevalence, trustworthiness ratings, and detailed speaker metadata. [MANDATORY]: Implemented XML tagging protocol (<thinking>, <research_methodology>, <instructions>) for structural integrity.

Added an optional Jefferson transcription mode for high-rigor conversation analysis and restructured prompts around explicit XML tags while preserving denaturalized transcription as the default UX research workflow.
